popularStory.headline: Basic History
popularStory.description: Robert de was born circa 1036 at Tosni, Normandy, France. He later became known as Robert de Stafford. He was the son of Roger de Tosni and Adelaide Borrell of Barcelona. Robert married Adelisa de Sav …

zh.lifeSummary: 當Robert de Tosny de Stafford於從1030年到1036年出生在英國萊希斯特斯爾貝爾沃易 卡素時，他的父親Robert de Tosny I of Belvoir40歲，母親Adèle Osule28歲。他和Adelisa de Savona至少有5個兒子和2個女兒。他去世於1088年8月4日在英國英格蘭萊希斯特斯爾貝爾沃易 卡素，埋葬在英國萊希斯特斯爾貝爾沃易 卡素。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Robert de Tosny de Stafford于从1030年到1036年出生在英国萊希斯特斯爾貝爾沃易 卡素时，他的父亲Robert de Tosny I of Belvoir40岁，母亲Adèle Osule28岁。他和Adelisa de Savona至少有5个儿子和2个女儿。他去世于1088年8月4日在英国英格兰萊希斯特斯爾貝爾沃易 卡素，埋葬在英国萊希斯特斯爾貝爾沃易 卡素。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Tosny
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Andre, Jean Pierre.
Americanized form of any of various surnames ultimately derived from the Latin personal name Antonius (see Anthony ), for example Greek Antoniou and Italian Toni .
French: from the personal name Tony, a pet form of Antonin (from Latin Antoninus, a derivative of Antonius; see Anthony ).

variants.name: Anthony
meanings: English and West Indian (mainly Antigua and Barbuda, Saint Lucia, and Trinidad and Tobago, also Dutch Caribbean): from the personal name Anthony, Latin Antonius, which, with its variants and cognates, is one of the commonest personal names in Europe. Spellings with -h-, which first appear in English in the 16th century and in French (as Anthoine) at about the same time, are due to the erroneous belief that the name derives from Greek anthos ‘flower’. The popularity of the personal name in Christendom is largely due to the cult of the Egyptian hermit Saint Anthony ( AD 251–356), who in his old age gathered a community of hermits around him, and for that reason is regarded by some as the founder of monasticism. It was further increased by the fame of Saint Anthony of Padua (1195–1231), who long enjoyed a great popular cult and who is believed to help people find lost things. In North America, the English form of the surname has absorbed cognates and derivatives (patronymics) from other languages, for example Greek patronymic Antoniades , Italian Antoni , Polish Antoniewicz , Croatian and Serbian Antonović (see Antonovich ) and Antunović; see also below. The name Anthony is also found among Christians in southern India, but since South Indians traditionally do not have hereditary surnames, the southern Indian name was in most cases registered as such only after immigration of its bearers to the US. Compare Antony .

German, Flemish, and French (mainly Alsace): Latinized (humanistic) patronymic from local equivalents of the Latin personal name Antonius, from its genitive form Antoni(i). In North America, this surname is also an altered form of the German, Dutch, French, and Slovak cognates Antoni 1 and Antony 2.
History: John Anthony of Hampstead, Middlesex, England (now part of north London) migrated to Boston, MA, in 1634. By 1640 he had moved to Providence, RI, where his descendants are still established.

nameMeanings.name: Robert
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: One of the many French names of Germanic origin that were introduced into Britain by the Normans; it has since remained in continuous use. It is derived from the nearly synonymous elements hrōd ‘fame’ + berht ‘bright, famous’, and had a native Old English predecessor of similar form (Hreodbeorht), which was supplanted by the Norman name. Two dukes of Normandy in the 11th century bore the name: the father of William the Conqueror (sometimes identified with the legendary Robert the Devil), and his eldest son. It was borne also by three kings of Scotland, notably Robert the Bruce ( 1274–1329 ), who freed Scotland from English domination. The altered short form Bob is very common, but Hob and Dob, which were common in the Middle Ages and gave rise to surnames, are extinct. See also Rupert .

historicEvents.title: England Divided
historicEvents.description: King Canute died in 1035 and England was divided between his three sons. His son Sweyn received Norway, Harthacanute received Denmark, and Harold Harefoot received North England.

historicEvents.title: Battle of Hastings
historicEvents.description: Battle of Hastings was fought between the Norman-French army and the battle-wary English army on October 14, 1066. Duke William of Normandy believed he was the rightful king rather than Harold, who had been crowned king several months earlier. Harold was killed in battle and William was crowned king on Christmas Day 1066.

historicEvents.title: Domesday Book
historicEvents.description: Domesday Book is Britain's earliest extant public record with information containing land surveys and landholdings commissioned by William I. The book was completed in 1086.

en.lifeSummary: When Robert de Tosny de Stafford was born from 1030 to 1036, in Belvoir Castle, Leicestershire, England, his father, Robert de Tosny I of Belvoir, was 40 and his mother, Adèle Osule, was 28. He had at least 5 sons and 2 daughters with Adelisa de Savona. He died on 4 August 1088, in Belvoir Castle, Leicestershire, England, United Kingdom, and was buried in Belvoir Castle, Leicestershire, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Robert de Tosny de Stafford est né du 1030 au 1036, à Belvoir Castle, Leicestershire, Angleterre, son père, Robert de Tosny I of Belvoir, avait 40 ans et sa mère, Adèle Osule, avait 28 ans. Il a eu au moins 5 fils et 2 filles avec Adelisa de Savona. Il est décédé le 4 août 1088, à Belvoir Castle, Leicestershire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, et a été enterré à Belvoir Castle, Leicestershire, Angleterre.

de.lifeSummary: Als Robert de Tosny de Stafford von 1030 bis 1036 in Belvoir Castle, Leicestershire, England geboren wurde, war sein Vater Robert de Tosny I of Belvoir 40 und seine Mutter Adèle Osule 28. Er hatte mit Adelisa de Savona mindestens 5 Söhne und 2 Töchter. Er starb am 4. August 1088 in Belvoir Castle, Leicestershire, England, United Kingdom und wurde in Belvoir Castle, Leicestershire, England begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Robert de Tosny de Stafford è nato il da 1030 a 1036 nel Belvoir Castle, Leicestershire, England, il suo padre, Robert de Tosny I of Belvoir, aveva 40 anni e il suo madre, Adèle Osule, aveva 28 anni. Lui avuto almeno 5 figli e 2 figlie con Adelisa de Savona. Morì il 4 agosto 1088, nel Belvoir Castle, Leicestershire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, e fu sepolto a Belvoir Castle, Leicestershire, England.

ja.lifeSummary: Robert de Tosny de Staffordがから1030年まで1036年にイギリスレアイセステルシュアイアベルボイル カストルで生まれた時，父Robert de Tosny I of Belvoirは40歳，母Adèle Osuleは28歳であった。彼はAdelisa de Savonaとの間に少なくとも5人の息子と2人の娘をもうけた。彼は1088年8月4日にイギリスイギリスレアイセステルシュアイアベルボイル カストルで死去し，イギリスレアイセステルシュアイアベルボイル カストルにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Robert de Tosny de Stafford(이)가 1030년 부터 1036년 까지 영국 레이스스터실르 벨보이르 카스틀에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Robert de Tosny I of Belvoir(은)는 40세였고, 그의 어머니 Adèle Osule(은)는 28세였다. 그는 Adelisa de Savona(와)과 적어도 다섯 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1088년 8월 4일 영국 잉글랜드 레이스스터실르 벨보이르 카스틀에서 사망했으며 영국 레이스스터실르 벨보이르 카스틀에 묻혔다.

lv.From 1030 to 1036–4 August 1088: no 1030. gada līdz 1036. gadam–1088. gada 4. augusts

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Robert de Tosny de Stafford nasceu de 1030 a 1036, em Belvoir Castle, Leicestershire, Inglaterra, seu pai, Robert de Tosny I of Belvoir, tinha 40 anos e sua mãe, Adèle Osule, tinha 28 anos. Ele teve pelo menos 5 filhos e 2 filhas com Adelisa de Savona. Ele faleceu em 4 de agosto de 1088, em Belvoir Castle, Leicestershire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, e foi sepultado em Belvoir Castle, Leicestershire, Inglaterra.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Robert de Tosny de Stafford родился с 1030 по 1036 в местности белвоир кастл, лисестиршир, Англия, его отцу по имени Robert de Tosny I of Belvoir было 40 лет, а матери, которую звали Adèle Osule, 28. У него было по крайней мере 5 сыновей и 2 дочери от Adelisa de Savona. Он умер 4 августа 1088 г. в местности белвоир кастл, лисестиршир, Англия, Великобритания и был похоронен в местности белвоир кастл, лисестиршир, Англия.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Robert de Tosny de Stafford nació desde 1030 hasta 1036, en Belvoir Castle, Leicestershire, Inglaterra, su papá, Robert de Tosny I of Belvoir, tenía 40 años y su mamá, Adèle Osule, tenía 28 años. Tuvo por lo menos 5 hijos y 2 hijas con Adelisa de Savona. Murió el 4 de agosto de 1088, en Belvoir Castle, Leicestershire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, y fue sepultado en Belvoir Castle, Leicestershire, Inglaterra.
